questions.text: What is the primary role of Node.js in blockchain development?

options.text: A JavaScript runtime that executes deployment scripts, tests, and development tools like Hardhat.
A package manager for smart contracts.
A code editor for writing Solidity.

questions.explanation: Node.js is the runtime environment for JavaScript outside the browser. Hardhat, ethers.js, and deployment scripts all run on Node.js. npm is the package manager; VS Code is the editor.
questions.modelAnswer:
✅ Correct: B

Node.js is the runtime environment for JavaScript outside the browser. Hardhat, ethers.js, and deployment scripts all run on Node.js. npm is the package manager; VS Code is the editor.

questions.text: Which command initializes a new npm project and creates a package.json file?
questions.explanation: npm init -y creates a default package.json with project metadata. The -y flag skips the interactive questionnaire.
questions.modelAnswer:
✅ Correct: B

npm init -y creates a default package.json with project metadata. The -y flag skips the interactive questionnaire.

questions.text: Which VS Code extension is essential for Solidity syntax highlighting and error diagnostics?
questions.explanation: The "Solidity" extension by Juan Blanco provides syntax highlighting, compilation, and error diagnostics for .sol files. Prettier formats code; ESLint lints JavaScript; GitLens enhances Git.
questions.modelAnswer:
✅ Correct: A

The "Solidity" extension by Juan Blanco provides syntax highlighting, compilation, and error diagnostics for .sol files. Prettier formats code; ESLint lints JavaScript; GitLens enhances Git.

questions.text: What does the caret (^) prefix mean in a package.json version like "ethers": "^6.13.0"?

options.text: Install exactly version 6.13.0, no other version allowed.
Allow updates that do not change the leftmost non-zero digit - any 6.x.x version.
Install the latest beta version of the package.
Install the global version of the package.

questions.explanation: The caret (^) allows minor and patch updates. ^6.13.0 means >=6.13.0 and <7.0.0. This follows Semantic Versioning (SemVer).
questions.modelAnswer:
✅ Correct: B

The caret (^) allows minor and patch updates. ^6.13.0 means >=6.13.0 and <7.0.0. This follows Semantic Versioning (SemVer).

questions.text: After installing Node.js, which two terminal commands confirm the installation was successful?
questions.explanation: node -v prints the Node.js version; npm -v prints the npm version. If both show version numbers, the installation is correct.
questions.modelAnswer:
✅ Correct: A

node -v prints the Node.js version; npm -v prints the npm version. If both show version numbers, the installation is correct.
